ccel.title: NPNF2-09. Hilary of Poitiers, John of Damascus

source.title: NPNF2-09. Hilary of Poitiers, John of Damascus

title: Concerning the Holy Spirit, a reasoned proof.

source.title: NPNF2-09. Hilary of Poitiers, John of Damascus

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Moreover the Word must also possess Spirit.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For in fact even our word is not destitute of spirit; but in our case the spirit is something different from our essence.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For there is an attraction and movement of the air which is drawn in and poured forth that the body may be sustained.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: And it is this which in the moment of utterance becomes the articulate word, revealing in itself the force of the word.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: But in the case of the divine nature, which is simple and uncompound, we must confess in all piety that there exists a Spirit of God, for the Word is not more imperfect than our own word.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Now we cannot, in piety, consider the Spirit to be something foreign that gains admission into God from without, as is the case with compound natures like us.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Nay, just as, when we heard of the Word of God, we considered it to be not without subsistence, nor the product of learning, nor the mere utterance of voice, nor as passing into the air and perishing, but as being essentially subsisting, endowed with free volition, and energy, and omnipotence: so also, when we have learnt about the Spirit of God, we contemplate it as the companion of the Word and the revealer of His energy, and not as mere breath without subsistence.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For to conceive of the Spirit that dwells in God as after the likeness of our own spirit, would be to drag down the greatness of the divine nature to the lowest depths of degradation.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: But we must contemplate it as an essential power, existing in its own proper and peculiar subsistence, proceeding from the Father and resting in the Word, and shewing forth the Word, neither capable of disjunction from God in Whom it exists, and the Word Whose companion it is, nor poured forth to vanish into nothingness, but being in subsistence in the likeness of the Word, endowed with life, free volition, independent movement, energy, ever willing that which is good, and having power to keep pace with the will in all its decrees, having no beginning and no end.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For never was the Father at any time lacking in the Word, nor the Word in the Spirit.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Thus because of the unity in nature, the error of the Greeks in holding that God is many, is utterly destroyed: and again by our acceptance of the Word and the Spirit, the dogma of the Jews is overthrown: and there remains of each party only what is profitable.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: On the one hand of the Jewish idea we have the unity of God’s nature, and on the other, of the Greek, we have the distinction in subsistences and that only.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: But should the Jew refuse to accept the Word and the Spirit, let the divine Scripture confute him and curb his tongue.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For concerning the Word, the divine David says, For ever, O Lord, Thy Word is settled in heaven.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: And again, He sent His Word and healed them.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: But the word that is uttered is not sent, nor is it for ever settled.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: And concerning the Spirit, the same David says, Thou sendest forth Thy Spirit, they are created.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: And again, By the word of the Lord were the heavens made: and all the host of them by the breath of His mouth.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Job, too, says, The Spirit of God hath made me, and the breath of the Almighty hath given me life.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Now the Spirit which is sent and makes and stablishes and conserves, is not mere breath that dissolves, any more than the mouth of God is a bodily member.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For the conception of both must be such as harmonizes with the Divine nature.
